data.title: Businesses embrace Bitcoin amid 2025 bull market surge – River
data.description:
Businesses have become a driving force in Bitcoin’s 2025 bull market, with corporate holdings now accounting for more than 6% of its total supply, according to a new report from River Financial. The report found that in the first eight months of 2025 alone, business inflows into Bitcoin surpassed last year’s total by $12.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/businesses-embrace-bitcoin-amid-2025-bull-market-surge-river/

data.title: Fed's Kashkari: There is good reason to believe the economy will cool moderately and there is room for a moderate reduction in interest rates
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kashkari: There is good reason to believe that the economy will cool moderately, and there is room for a moderate reduction in interest rates. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Beige Book: Most districts report economic stagnation
data.description: The Federal Reserve Beige Book: Economic activity in most Federal Reserve districts showed little or no change since the last report, with four districts reporting moderate growth. Ten districts said price increases were moderate or modest. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Justice Department Defends Trump's Decision to Dismiss Fed Governor Lisa Cook
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Department of Justice has presented new arguments supporting U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to dismiss Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, challenging her claims that the dismissal was a pretext for lowering interest rates. Cook is accused of mortgage fraud and is contesting her removal. On Thursday, government attorneys urged a judge to reject Cook's request to prevent her dismissal during the lawsuit, reinforcing arguments made at last week's hearing.

The legal documents were submitted hours after reports emerged that the Justice Department had launched a criminal investigation into Cook. The government argues that the fraud allegations, initially raised by Federal Housing Finance Agency Director Pulte, provide sufficient grounds under U.S. law for Trump to dismiss her.

The Justice Department emphasized in Thursday's filing that the judge should not question Trump's judgment regarding the grounds for dismissal, dismissing claims that the removal was a pretext to control the Federal Reserve and lower interest rates. "Her only 'evidence' is the President's criticism of Federal Reserve policies," the document states, "but mere policy disagreements do not imply that the President dismissed Cook for that reason."

data.title: Fed Nominee Milan Denies Discussing Long-Term Term with White House
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump's nominee for the Federal Reserve Board, Stephen Milan, stated on Thursday that he has not engaged in discussions with anyone at the White House regarding a potential long-term appointment. This statement comes after he informed senators that he would only resign from his current role as a White House economic advisor if nominated for a long-term position, rather than taking a temporary leave.

When pressed by reporters on the matter, Milan initially raised an eyebrow and declined to comment. Upon further questioning, he briefly responded, "No." During Milan's nomination hearing, Democratic senators expressed concerns that his decision not to resign from the economic advisory role could lead to undue influence from President Trump on interest rate decisions. Milan denied these allegations.

data.title: Top US Securities Regulator Announces Sweeping Review of Crypto Oversight and Financial Disclosure: Report
data.description:
The U. S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has revealed its upcoming rulemaking agenda, which includes extensive proposals to modernize cryptocurrency regulations and ease what many in the financial industry have called unnecessarily heavy compliance burdens, according to a new Reuters report
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/09/04/top-us-securities-regulator-announces-sweeping-review-of-crypto-oversight-and-financial-disclosure-report/
